_comment: Single source of truth for the SwingVantage Admin Dashboard How-To Guide PDF. Edit this file, then re-run capture + build (see README.md). Strings may contain <b>/<i> inline markup.

subtitle: A complete, illustrated walkthrough of every section of the
SwingVantage admin — written for non-developers.

intro.heading: What this dashboard is
paras: This is the control center for running SwingVantage — one place to see what is happening, manage content, review AI output, configure each sport, watch system health and keep the platform safe. Think of it like the WordPress or Shopify admin, but built for an AI sports-analysis product.
bullets: Privacy-first & local-first. Each user’s data lives in their own account, and swing videos are processed on the device and never uploaded. The admin shows metadata about analyses, never the video files.

Cross-user numbers need one key. To count users and sessions across everyone, the dashboard uses a server-side key (the service-role key). Without it you see honest ‘Local mode’ prompts instead of invented numbers.
Keyless-first. Ads, billing, AI and email all work in a safe fallback with no keys, and ‘upgrade’ when you add one. The Integrations page shows what is connected.
intro.after: New here? Open the Command Center, read the alert cards, then use the sidebar. The Admin Academy has guided walkthroughs.

intro.heading: How to get in
paras: The whole admin area is protected. You get in one of two ways:
bullets: Allowlisted email (recommended). Log in normally; if your email is on the admin allowlist, you are an admin.
Secret header. Trusted tools or proxies can pass a matching secret header.
intro.after: In local development with neither set, the admin is open so you can build. In production with neither set, nobody gets in (secure by default). Non-admins are quietly sent to the dashboard and never told the route exists.

intro.heading: Roles & permissions
paras: Anyone who clears the front door is a Super Admin by default. You can narrow this with finer roles — Content Manager, SEO Manager, Support Agent, AI Review Specialist, Sports Content Editor, Monetization Manager, Analyst or Read-Only Viewer. Roles are set on the server, and narrowing only ever removes capability — the browser can never grant itself access. Every change is written to the Audit Log.

sections.title: 1. Command Center
sections.blurb: Your home base. A live executive overview: smart alert cards surface anything that needs attention, key metrics summarize the platform at a glance, and a recent-activity feed shows the latest admin actions. Start here every time — read the alerts, then jump into a section from the sidebar.

sections.title: 2. Users
sections.blurb: Every account in one searchable table. Click any user to open their full journey — profile, sports, activity and history — and from there export their data or suspend/restore the account. Swing videos never appear here: they are processed on the device and never uploaded.

sections.title: 3. Athletes
sections.blurb: A directory of every (user + sport) profile across all 7 sports. One person can be several athletes — for example golf and tennis — so this is the fastest way to see who is active in each sport and open a profile for detail.

sections.title: 4. Uploads & Media
sections.blurb: Analysis metadata only — sport, score and timing. By privacy design the actual swing videos are never uploaded to our servers, so there are no video files to browse. This page honours that promise rather than hiding it.

sections.title: 5. AI Analyses
sections.blurb: Review queues built from analysis scores, plus a per-analysis detail view. Use it to spot-check the quality of AI output and catch anything that looks off before it can erode user trust.

sections.title: 6. Content
sections.blurb: The content hub: counts and quick links for your blog posts, SEO pages and sport content. It is the jumping-off point for everything you publish.

sections.title: 7. Generated Fixes
sections.blurb: A review queue for AI-drafted ‘fix’ content. Nothing here is ever auto-published — items pass a relevance gate and then wait for your explicit approval. Review, then publish or discard.

sections.title: 8. SEO / AEO / GEO
sections.blurb: Your live SEO catalog. Every page is scored for structure and ‘answer-readiness’ (how well it can be quoted by AI search and answer engines), so you can see which pages are strong and which need work.

sections.title: 9. Sports
sections.blurb: Per-sport analysis configuration pulled from the core sport registry. See how each of the 7 sports is set up, and click a sport to open its detailed configuration.

sections.title: 10. Sports — detail (Golf)
sections.blurb: An example of a single sport’s configuration. Each sport — golf, tennis, pickleball, padel, baseball, softball and slow-pitch — has its own page describing how its analysis behaves.

sections.title: 11. Monetization
sections.blurb: Ad surfaces and the UX safety rules that protect the free experience. SwingVantage grows the free audience first, so this page keeps advertising honest and non-intrusive.

sections.title: 12. Analytics
sections.blurb: The catalog of tracked events and key funnels. Live counts appear once an analytics provider is connected; until then the catalog shows exactly what is measured — never invented numbers.

sections.title: 13. Support
sections.blurb: A ticket queue with the user and sport context attached, so you can answer questions quickly with the full picture in front of you.

sections.title: 14. Feedback
sections.blurb: Typed user feedback flowing into a roadmap pipeline — a simple way to turn what users say into prioritized product decisions.

sections.title: 15. Notifications
sections.blurb: A derived alert center and activity feed: the important signals from across the admin, gathered into one place so nothing slips by.

sections.title: 16. System Health
sections.blurb: Plain-English status for every service the platform depends on. Green means good; anything else is explained in everyday language so you know what to do.

sections.title: 17. Integrations
sections.blurb: What is connected and what is not — Supabase, AI, email, billing, ads and more. It shows only whether a key is present, never the secret itself. After adding a key, press Re-check.

sections.title: 18. Feature Flags
sections.blurb: Turn features on or off, roll them out to a percentage of users or to specific segments, and roll back instantly. High-risk toggles ask for confirmation first.

sections.title: 19. Audit Log
sections.blurb: The searchable, exportable history of every meaningful admin change — who did what, the before and after, and when. This is your accountability record.

sections.title: 20. Security & Roles
sections.blurb: Your security posture at a glance, the permission matrix for each role, and an email-to-role overlay for assigning finer permissions. The server always enforces roles; this overlay can only narrow access, never grant it.

sections.title: 21. Legal & Privacy
sections.blurb: Links to your policies plus data-rights workflows (export and delete) for handling user privacy requests responsibly.

sections.title: 22. Settings
sections.blurb: An honest map of how the platform is configured — which settings come from environment variables and which are set in code. No hidden surprises.

sections.title: 23. Admin Academy
sections.blurb: Your onboarding checklist, step-by-step playbooks and a glossary. New to the admin? Start here for guided walkthroughs.

sections.title: 24. Growth & built-in products
sections.blurb: The gateway to the larger built-in tools: GrowthOS (omnichannel marketing), AdsOS, Video Studio, Social, Insights, Re-engage and the Staff Academy. Each is a product in its own right with its own screens — and its own documentation in the /docs folder.

security.heading: Security best practices
bullets: The UI is never trusted alone — every admin action is re-checked on the server.
The service-role key (which can bypass row-level security) is used only in server code that has already verified you.
Secrets are never shown in the browser — integration pages reveal only whether a key is present.
Dangerous actions (deleting a user or media, publishing AI content, toggling a core flag, editing legal pages, bulk operations) require a confirmation dialog and write an audit entry.

troubleshooting.heading: Troubleshooting
bullets: ‘Local mode’ or blank counts on the Command Center → add the service-role key (plus the public Supabase keys) and press Re-check on Integrations.
A section says ‘Soon’ → it is planned for a later wave, not broken.
Redirected to the dashboard when opening the admin → your email is not on the admin allowlist (or you are not logged in). Add it and log in again.
An integration shows ‘Off’ but you set the key → the app reads settings at startup; restart or redeploy, then Re-check.

about.heading: About this guide
about.text: Screenshots were captured from a live build on {date}. Some data-driven pages may look sparse because the build had little sample data — in production, with the service-role key set, those pages populate with your real numbers. For the larger built-in products linked from the sidebar (GrowthOS, AdsOS, Video Studio, Social, Insights, Re-engage, Staff Academy), see their dedicated documents in the /docs folder. This guide regenerates itself: see scripts/admin-guide/README.md.
